title: Kid Rock Buries Bad Bunny in TMZ's Post-Super Bowl Halftime Show Poll 31 Chris Graythen/Getty ImagesWarner Todd Huston11
raw_response: {"rating": 5.5, "lean": "Right", "explanation": "This is a news report by framing but functions as opinion/editorial content. The author consistently celebrates Kid Rock (a politically conservative figure aligned with Trump) while disparaging Bad Bunny (described as 'rabidly anti-Trump'), and frames the TMZ poll result as a culture-war victory. The strong, consistent right-leaning framing throughout warrants a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes Bad Bunny as a 'rabidly anti-Trump rapper,' an intensely loaded characterization that frames opposition to Trump negatively and signals right-leaning sympathies. (2) The headline uses the combative verb 'Buries' to celebrate Kid Rock's poll victory, framing it as a decisive cultural triumph rather than a simple poll result. (3) The author accuses TMZ of deliberate bias, claiming TMZ 'tried its best to lead its fans to pick Bad Bunny' and that the site 'frontloaded the poll' — these are the author's own editorial characterizations presented as fact without evidence of intent. (4) The phrase 'Fans did not fall for the push' frames TMZ's coverage as manipulative and the poll result as a righteous rejection of that manipulation, reinforcing a right-leaning media-distrust narrative. (5) The author notes Bad Bunny wiped his social media accounts as a 'strange turn of events,' implying something embarrassing or suspicious without providing context, adding to the negative framing of the anti-Trump figure. (6) The article is tagged with 'Turning Point USA,' a right-wing organization, and the overall framing aligns with right-leaning culture-war narratives celebrating conservative-aligned figures over liberal-aligned ones."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report by framing but functions as opinion/editorial content. The author consistently celebrates Kid Rock (a politically conservative figure aligned with Trump) while disparaging Bad Bunny (described as 'rabidly anti-Trump'), and frames the TMZ poll result as a culture-war victory. The strong, consistent right-leaning framing throughout warrants a Right classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes Bad Bunny as a 'rabidly anti-Trump rapper,' an intensely loaded characterization that frames opposition to Trump negatively and signals right-leaning sympathies. (2) The headline uses the combative verb 'Buries' to celebrate Kid Rock's poll victory, framing it as a decisive cultural triumph rather than a simple poll result. (3) The author accuses TMZ of deliberate bias, claiming TMZ 'tried its best to lead its fans to pick Bad Bunny' and that the site 'frontloaded the poll' — these are the author's own editorial characterizations presented as fact without evidence of intent. (4) The phrase 'Fans did not fall for the push' frames TMZ's coverage as manipulative and the poll result as a righteous rejection of that manipulation, reinforcing a right-leaning media-distrust narrative. (5) The author notes Bad Bunny wiped his social media accounts as a 'strange turn of events,' implying something embarrassing or suspicious without providing context, adding to the negative framing of the anti-Trump figure. (6) The article is tagged with 'Turning Point USA,' a right-wing organization, and the overall framing aligns with right-leaning culture-war narratives celebrating conservative-aligned figures over liberal-aligned ones.
